meta.workDescription: To ensure medical fit and deployable forces. To provide expeditionary medics, providers, and medical personnel to serve in deployed environments.  To improve the health of all we serve.
meta.references:
AFI 48-109 Electromagnetic Field Radiation OEH Program
AFI 48-137 Respiratory Protection Program
AFI 48-139 Laser and Optical Radiation Protection Program
DAFMAN 91-203
AF Form 2767
OSHA 2254
29 CFR 1910.134
meta.hazardAnalysis: Hazards in the MDG include sharps/needle sticks, biohazard waste, ergonomic lifting, tripping, ladders, electrical shock/fire, outdore exposure to thermal stress, and typical office-related threats to personal health and safety.
meta.riskManagementNotes: Hazards are identified, risks are assessed, controls are developed and implemented, and supervisors evaluate and validate successful mitigation.  Members are trained to incorporate risk management techniques in all aspects of their on-/off-duty activities.
meta.trafficSafetyNotes:
- Use caution when entering and exiting the main gate barriers
- Park in approved spots only
- Follow base speed limits
- Use caution when walking, jogging, or biking on the roadways
- When driving, ensure safe distances and clearances from people and other vehicles
meta.motorcycleSafetyReps: TSgt Harrigan (BE office, rm 16) is the Motorcycle Safety Representative.
meta.emergencyNumbers:
(Program these numbers into your phone, as needed)
- In case of an emergency, call 911.  be sure to know your location to instruct dispatch.
- BDOC emergency number - 812-877-5249
- BDOC Non-Emergency - 812-877-5727
meta.medicalFacility: There is an urgent care facility on south 46, in front of Walmart and behind Sonic.  After hours emergencies should go to Union Hospital's emergency room.
meta.evacuation:
MDG emergency locations:
- Bathrooms for tornadoes
- Classroom for chemical attack
- Outdoor pavilion by basketball court for fire rally point
meta.activeShooter:
- Report suspicious activities to BDOC
- Immediately lock yourself/others into a secure room, lock and/or block entrance(s)
- Follow emergency directions, assist injured if applicable
- Do not let unknown individuals into your secure area
- Do not look suspicious when security forces respond to your location
meta.weatherShelter: The building restrooms are designated shelter areas.
meta.bulletinBoard: The Safety board is outside the bathroom area by office 16.  The halls and breakroom are decorated with training and safety aids, and should be reviewed when time is available.
meta.emergencyEquipment:
Everyone should familiarize themselves with the following:
- There are four fire extinguishers in the building
- There is a building fire alarm by each exit
- There is a PA system by the exit near the immunization room (Key activated)
- There are three eyewash stations throughout the building (In the lab areas)

selectedModules.title: Bloodborne Pathogens
selectedModules.trainingRequirement: Train exposed employees at initial assignment and at least annually, with additional training when tasks or procedures create new exposure. Cover disease signs and transmission, the exposure control plan, task recognition, engineering controls, work practices, PPE use and disposal, and Hepatitis B vaccine information.
selectedModules.afTrainingRequirement: Any person whose duties involve reasonably anticipated exposure to blood or other potentially infectious material must be trained and enrolled in the Bloodborne Pathogen Program. Supervisors of affected workplaces must establish a written exposure control plan and conduct training.

selectedModules.title: CPR Training
selectedModules.trainingRequirement: Where work conditions require trained first-aid responders, ensure personnel are adequately trained and available. OSHA 2254 links first-aid and CPR availability to certain work settings, but local mission risk and AF requirements should define who must hold current CPR credentials.
selectedModules.afTrainingRequirement: Initial first aid and CPR training must be completed before assigning duties that require it, refresher training must occur before certification expires, and CPR training must include PAD instruction. Remote or high-risk work must have an immediate medical response plan.

selectedModules.title: Electromagnetic Field Training
selectedModules.trainingRequirement: Use AF EMF program guidance for source identification, posted restrictions, exposure boundaries, and work controls. The closest OSHA 2254 crosswalk is electrical safety training for employees exposed to shock or electrical hazards and the electric power standard’s job-assignment-specific safety practices and emergency procedures.
selectedModules.afTrainingRequirement: Employees with implanted medical devices must notify their supervisors before work and seek medical assessment before duties in EMF environments. Use AF EMF program controls and local work restrictions as the training basis.

selectedModules.title: Respiratory Protection Program
selectedModules.trainingRequirement: Provide comprehensive, understandable respirator training before use and at least annually thereafter. Cover why the respirator is needed, limitations, emergency use, inspection, donning and seal checks, maintenance and storage, and symptoms that may limit safe use.
selectedModules.afTrainingRequirement: Respiratory hazards must be evaluated by Bioenvironmental Engineering and respiratory protection used where required by the occupational health assessment. Use the AF Respiratory Protection Program for controls, fit testing, and local training requirements.
